actions.name: AINEX_BOW

actions.description: Run the learned `pick_up` policy. Starts a policy.start with task='pick_up'; options: target_label (default 'red ball'), max_steps.

actions.name: AINEX_PLACE_DOWN
actions.description: Run the learned `place_down` policy. Starts a policy.start with task='place_down'.

actions.description: Play a named Hiwonder action group (pre-recorded multi-servo motion). Options: name (required, must match a key in the profile's actions.groups).

actions.name: AINEX_RUN_RL
actions.description: Run a text-conditioned learned policy on the AiNex. Pass `options.text`

actions.name: AINEX_SET_SERVO
actions.description: Drive one or more AiNex servos to target pulse positions over a duration. Options: positions=[{id, position}], duration (seconds, default 0.5).

actions.name: AINEX_SIDE_STEP_LEFT
actions.description: Strafe the AiNex robot to its left. Fire-and-forget — robot walks until AINEX_STOP.

actions.name: AINEX_SIDE_STEP_RIGHT
actions.description: Strafe the AiNex robot to its right. Fire-and-forget — robot walks until AINEX_STOP.

actions.name: AINEX_SIT
actions.description: Play the `sit` action group — moves the AiNex into a seated pose.

actions.name: AINEX_STAND
actions.description: Play the `stand` action group — moves the AiNex into its calibrated home pose.

actions.name: AINEX_STOP
actions.description: Stop the AiNex robot immediately. Sends walk.command:stop with preempt=true so any in-flight commands or active policy are cleared.

actions.name: AINEX_TURN_LEFT
actions.description: Turn the AiNex robot in place to its left (positive yaw). Fire-and-forget — robot keeps turning until AINEX_STOP.

actions.name: AINEX_TURN_RIGHT
actions.description: Turn the AiNex robot in place to its right (negative yaw). Fire-and-forget — robot keeps turning until AINEX_STOP.

actions.name: AINEX_WALK_BACKWARD
actions.description: Start walking the AiNex robot backward. Sends walk.set+walk.command:start to the bridge; robot keeps walking until AINEX_STOP. Options: speed (1-4).

actions.name: AINEX_WALK_FORWARD
actions.description: Start walking the AiNex robot forward. Sends walk.set+walk.command:start to the bridge; the robot keeps walking until AINEX_STOP is issued. Options: speed (1-4), x (0-0.05).

actions.name: AINEX_WAVE
actions.description: Play the `wave` action group on the AiNex robot.

actions.name: ALARM
actions.description: Manage native macOS alarms via UNUserNotificationCenter. Subactions: set (schedule a new alarm), cancel (remove a scheduled alarm by id), list (show pending alarms). Pass the operation as the structured `action` parameter; when omitted it is inferred from the other structured params (a schedule payload → set, an id → cancel, otherwise list).

parameters.description: Canonical operation discriminator: set, cancel, or list. Legacy subaction/op aliases are still accepted.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Operation to perform: set, cancel, or list. Inferred from the other structured parameters when omitted.

parameters.name: timeIso
parameters.description: For subaction=set: ISO-8601 timestamp when the alarm should fire.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: For subaction=set: short title displayed in the notification.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: For subaction=set: optional longer body text for the notification.

parameters.name: sound
parameters.description: For subaction=set: optional notification sound name.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: For subaction=set: optional explicit alarm id; for subaction=cancel: required alarm id returned from a previous set operation.

actions.name: COMPUTER_USE_AGENT
actions.description: computer_use_agent: autonomous desktop loop for a goal until done or maxSteps. Uses WS6 scene-builder, WS7 Brain+Actor cascade, WS5 multi-monitor coords. parameters.name: streamProgress
parameters.description: When true, emit a chat callback after each dispatched step with compact progress and the step kind/rationale.

parameters.name: maxDurationMs
parameters.description: Wall-clock budget in ms; the loop aborts before a step that exceeds it.

parameters.name: imageRetentionLast
parameters.description: Keep only the N most-recent steps' screenshots in the bounded history (token control).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Autonomous desktop loop: scene -> Brain -> cascade -> click. Pass {goal, maxSteps?, streamProgress?}.

Prefer bounded commands; avoid recursive whole-filesystem scans unless explicitly requested. Omit cwd unless the user supplied an exact directory or the session was explicitly moved; do not invent cwd from remembered repo paths. For questions about the currently running agent/runtime/source, use the default session cwd and inspect current process/service evidence before reporting git metadata. For JSON API inspection, prefer jq or node; if Python is needed, call python3 rather than assuming a python alias exists. For public unauthenticated API reads, quote URLs and prefer stable no-key endpoints; avoid deprecated, region-blocked, or exchange-gated endpoints when a neutral data API can answer the same question. For crypto spot prices, prefer neutral no-key APIs such as CoinGecko simple price or Coinbase spot before exchange-gated APIs; do not start with legacy Coindesk or Binance when the same value can be fetched elsewhere. If a command exits 0 with empty stdout/stderr, the command produced no output; try another source or parser when data is still needed instead of claiming the shell did not return output. For disk checks, use df for every requested mount/path (for root plus home: df -h / /home) plus targeted du on likely cleanup directories; when asked for cleanup candidates, inspect one readable largest directory one level deeper before ranking candidates. Use separators that still allow later inspection commands to run when du hits expected permission-denied paths.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Shell operation: run | clear_history | view_history.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: For action=run: shell command, executed via /bin/bash -c. Keep routine inspection commands bounded; avoid broad scans like du -sh /* when a targeted path is enough. For JSON API data, prefer jq or node; use python3, not python, unless the environment explicitly shows python exists. For public unauthenticated API reads, quote URLs and prefer stable no-key endpoints; avoid deprecated, region-blocked, or exchange-gated endpoints when a neutral data API can answer the same question. For crypto spot prices, prefer CoinGecko simple price or Coinbase spot before exchange-gated APIs; avoid legacy Coindesk and Binance when a neutral source can answer. If stdout/stderr are marked empty, the command produced no output; try a different command/source when the user still needs a value. Include every requested path in df, e.g. df -h / /home. For cleanup candidates, follow the first bounded du result with a targeted du on the largest readable directory before answering; avoid && between du probes when permission-denied paths are expected.
